items.headline: Major commercial project breaks ground on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in San José's industrial corridor. This is the only substantial commercial investment in this permit batch.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses in shopping centers obtain proper licensing, coupled with outreach efforts and economic incentives for retail and small businesses. This could affect local shop owners and the viability of Cupertino's shopping districts.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual San José city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event with city funding. The city will also accept donations of materials and services to support the cleanup effort.

items.headline: City seeks community input on federal housing grant funding
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission heard presentations from applicants competing for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal dollars support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City considers new retail vitality rules to boost struggling commercial areas
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed proposed zoning and building code changes designed to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. The amendments could affect how retail spaces are regulated and developed throughout Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan explores new transportation and land use options
items.summary: Planners studied alternatives for the San Antonio Road area's future development and transportation network. This study session could shape how that corridor develops and functions over the coming years.

items.headline: Los Altos adopts Below Market Rate housing unit transfer exemption
items.summary: The city council approved a new policy on BMR (Below Market Rate) housing units, likely affecting how affordable housing can be transferred or sold. This could impact the availability and rules around Los Altos's affordable housing stock.

items.headline: Council reviews housing element implementation schedule for 2026
items.summary: The city presented a timeline for implementing its housing element, which typically includes plans for new housing development and zoning changes. This is relevant for residents interested in future growth, development, and housing availability in Los Altos.
